frameworks.name: probabilistic population coding
frameworks.one_line: neural circuits represent likelihoods of high-dimensional spike patterns

frameworks.name: random/sparse neural connectivity
frameworks.one_line: biologically inspired random projections enable scalable likelihood estimation

frameworks.name: statistical models of neural population activity
frameworks.one_line: existing approaches do not scale or lack biological realism

frameworks.name: deep learning density estimation
frameworks.one_line: current methods either fail to scale or lack realistic implementation

core_question: Can a biologically realistic, scalable neural model estimate the likelihood of high-dimensional spiking patterns?
key_assumptions: neural codes are noisy and spike-based
real cortical circuits have sparse and random connectivity
likelihood estimation is a basic building block for neural computation and learning
intrinsic neural noise can be exploited for learning
manipulations: model architecture inspired by random sparse connectivity
evaluation on simultaneous recordings of >100 neurons
tested across monkey visual and prefrontal cortex populations
varied training-sample size for learnability assessment
key_findings: model accurately estimates likelihood of individual spiking patterns
performance comparable to or better than current models on >100-neuron recordings
model can be learned from a small number of samples
local learning rule leverages noise intrinsic to neural circuits
interpretations: sparse random connectivity supports scalable probabilistic neural codes
biologically realistic likelihood estimation can underlie supervised and unsupervised learning
